scaffolds.q: For smooth movement, opposing muscles must be coordinated.
scaffolds.explain: Absolutely — and let me show you why. If both the biceps and triceps contracted at the same time with equal force, your elbow wouldn't move — it would be LOCKED in place, rigid and useless. Coordination is essential for function. we're going deeper.

scaffolds.q: Inhibitory interneurons can prevent motor neurons from firing.
scaffolds.explain: Yes — and Now we arrive at the gorgeous part:. Interneurons in the spinal cord can release inhibitory neurotransmitters (like glycine or GABA) that hyperpolarize motor neurons, preventing them from firing. This effectively relaxes the muscle they control — all at the reflex level. this is the mechanism that makes reciprocal inhibition possible.
